generated_comment: Arre yaar, every second word in Mumbai traffic, cricket, college groups is some mother-sister type gaali. It is not that deep every time; people use it for frustration, not because they literally mean anything about women. And please, calling every such word misogyny is too much — thoda mard bano, don't get offended like this over normal slang.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-body/chastity-rooted abusive language and dismisses concerns with 'it's just a word.' It also uses feminizing sensitivity as an insult ('mard bano'), which encodes the frame's masculinity-femininity hierarchy.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are commenting under your real name on a Facebook post where someone has shared an article about marital rape legislation. The comment section is divided. You are writing a detailed comment explaining why you think criminalising marital rape will destroy Indian marriages, why consent within marriage is a western concept that does not map onto Indian family structures, and what you think wives who use this law will use it for.
